feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Identity Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Identity Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Provision users in sac
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have two group in IAS: one group (sac_azure) provision from Azure and onw group (sac_ias) creted in IAS direkt.

How can I provision only this two groups to SAC? I have configured this  properties „sac.group.filter = displayName eq "sac_azure","sac_ias"   in the IAS Source System, But still, all users are provisioned to SAC.

Thanks in advance

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP IDM doesn't trigger Initial Load Job
entry.content:
Facing very strange issue:

We have imported AD package from Development to Quality IDM system, added AD repository details and while I run the initial load for AD, it doesn't trigger anything in Job Log. I confirm, dispatcher is running and SF Job & Process are running fine with same dispatcher.

Later I imported ABAP Package from Dev to QA IDM and there as well facing same issue with running Initial Load.

Then we created another dispatcher in QA IDM to match the name with Dev IDM and then re-imported both packages, unfortunately same issue.

In mc_jobs table, Jobdefinition is NOT NULL (some b64 encoded value) and statustext says - 'Error retrieving job definition'

Whereas under Initial Load Job properties, each time I try to run the job, it add Number of Errors -

Any help will be greatly appreciated?

-RP

entry.title: Error with provisioning of user
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When provisioning users from IAS to SAC and if I enable the property sac.api.version=2,I get this Error:

user:934-4666-2f324-324efe-2342,

system=Target_SAC_IAS,


error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.analytics.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema."},

content={"active":true,"displayName":"sac1","emails":[{"primary":true,"value":"sac1@lab.com"}],"groups":[{"value":"sddd-dd3e-sddd-ew-ssd"},{" sddd-dd3e-sddd-ew-ssd "}],"name":{"familyName":"sac1","givenName":"sac1"},"schemas":["urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"],"urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters":{"idpUserId":"sac1@lab.com"},"userName":"sac1@lab.com"}

second error:

error=Could not execute delete of group entities in target system Target_SAC_IAS as defined delete threshold of 2 is exceeded. 5 entities were not deleted.

What could be the reason for this?

Many thanks

entry.title: SAP IPS Read/Resync Job
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I'm still not entirely clear on this.

Specifically, when I make changes to user properties (e.g., the username has changed due to marriage) in Azure and then run the "READ" job in IAS, these changes are not provisioned to IAS. Only changes like creating a new group or user in Azure are provisioned to IAS.

However, when I run the "RESYNC" job, the new changes are transferred to IAS, but all attributes (including those I modified in IAS, as well as custom attributes) get overwritten.

How do you manage this?

Which jobs should be executed to ensure that the additional attributes I entered in IAS are not overwritten?

How can we provision only the changes from Azure?

How can we maintain attributes in IAS so that they are not overwritten during the next job?

Many Thanks for support and tips!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS/IPS: Automatically assign Role in SAC
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to automatically assign roles to SAC users using this code. The users are provisioned to SAC with IPS, and the groups SAC_ADMIN and SAC_MODELER are visible in SAC (with the users).

However, when the users log into SAC, the roles are not automatically assigned to them.

What might be wrong with the code?

{
    "user": {
        "condition": "($.emails[0].value EMPTY false) && isValidEmail($.emails[0].value)",
        "mappings": [
            {
                "constant": [
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters",
                    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"
                ],
                "targetPath": "$.schemas"
            },
            {
                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem",
                "targetPath": "$.id"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.emails[0].value",
                "targetPath": "$.userName"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value != []",
                "sourcePath": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": false,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.userName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.userName",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.userName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.name.givenName",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.name.givenName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.name.middleName",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.name.middleName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.name.familyName",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.name.familyName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.displayName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.externalId",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.externalId"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.active",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.active"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.emails",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "targetPath": "$.emails"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.emails[0].length() > 0",
                "constant": true,
                "targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.groups[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "resolveEntityIds",
                        "entityType": "group"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['value']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['value']",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "resolveEntityIds"
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    },
    "group": {
        "condition": "('%sac.group.prefix%' === 'null') || ($.displayName =~ /%sac.group.prefix%.*/)",
        "mappings": [
            {
                "constant": [
                    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group",
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles",
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters"
                ],
                "targetPath": "$.schemas"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.id",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "replaceFirstString",
                        "condition": "('%sac.group.prefix%' !== 'null') && (@ =~ /%sac.group.prefix%.*/)",
                        "regex": "%sac.group.prefix%",
                        "replacement": ""
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.displayName",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "replaceFirstString",
                        "condition": "('%sac.group.prefix%' !== 'null') && (@ =~ /%sac.group.prefix%.*/)",
                        "regex": "%sac.group.prefix%",
                        "replacement": ""
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.externalId",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.externalId"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.roles",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.roles"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "resolveEntityIds",
                        "entityType": "user"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:Group']['description']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']['description']"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.displayName == 'SAC_MODELER'",
                "constant": "PROFILE:sap.epm:Modeler",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']['roles'][0]['value']"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.displayName == 'SAC_MODELER'",
                "constant": "Modeler",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']['roles'][0]['display']"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.displayName == 'SAC_ADMIN'",
                "constant": "PROFILE:sap.epm:Admin",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']['roles'][1]['value']"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.displayName == 'SAC_ADMIN'",
                "constant": "Admin",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']['roles'][1]['display']"
            }
        ]
    }
}

 

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP IPS: Wrong value of schemas attribute
entry.content:
Helo Experts,

When I create the source system as IAS and the target system as SAC, provisioning to SAC works fine initially. However, when I make some changes or copy the code from the SAP documentation for SCIM API 2 and  a few errors appear - "Duplicated target paths warning and I try to resolve the errors, but I am unable to fix them.

Then, when I revert to the original transformation code (the one that worked at the beginning), I reinsert it, and provisioning stops working.

I get this error:

Caused by: Could not process successfully all entities from system with name: Source IAS

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema. Wrong value of schemas attribute"},

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Then nothing works anymore, and I have to delete the systems and create new ones.

Please help me understand how to avoid or fix such errors."

What am I doing wrong?

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Directory - Differences
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the difference between SAP Identity Authentication and Local Identity Directory in SAP IPS/IAS ?

In both cases, users are stored in SAP IAS, but I don't understand the difference and when each solution should be used.

Thank you very much!

 

BEst Regards

entry.title: Authorization to create CDS views
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can someone please provide if the below auth. are sufficient to create CDS view on HANA DB. If not, please rectify and/or suggest the correct ones

  • REPO.READ
  • REPO.EDIT_NATIVE_OBJECTS
  • REPO.ACTIVATE_NATIVE_OBJECTS
  • REPO.MAINTAIN_NATIVE_PACKAGESSystem privileges
  • REPO.MAINTAIN_DELIVERY_UNITSObject privileges
  • EXECUTE privilege on the database procedure SYS.REPOSITORY_REST.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: SAP Enable Now und IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured IAS as a proxy and am provisioning users from Azure to IAS and then to SAP Enable Now Azure->IAS->. How can roles be automatically assigned to provisioned users in Enable Now ?

Is there something similar to groups/teams in SAP Enable Now, like in SAC, that can be granted access via a role?

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on this.

Many Thanks for any help!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services and SAP ALM for business users
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM ; SAP Cloud Identity Services ; 

Dear SAP Community, 

I am working on getting more details and understanding of capabilities of SAP Cloud Identity Services through implementing cases focused on the integration of SAP Cloud Identity Services with local or customer identity providers, such as AD, ADFS, or local LDAP.

Currently, I would like to set the connection to SAP ALM for users who belong to a local identity provider (business users). We have many people who would like to work in ALM but I don't want to create manually the account for each user. The best is the user can login in ALM with the password of local identity provider and if the user belongs to the specific group in local Idp, then it should get a basic rights in ALM tenant. Specifically and most interesting, I am aiming to configure a scenario where users can log in to SAP ALM using their local identity provider passwords. From my understanding, there are three possible approaches:

  1. User provisioning operates in real-time, creating users upon their first connection to ALM. However, it appears that there is no target application related to ALM within IPS documentation.

  2. User provisioning operates in real-time, where the user is first created in IAS, with provisioning configured so that both source and target are set as IAS. A specific group can then be assigned to the user in IAS based on their group membership in the local identity provider. Using AD as a source in IPS is not good for us because we don't want to synch all AD users with IAS. *but may be we can use group as a filter to restrict the user list, however, AD as a source in IPS should be the last option if nothing else is available.

  3. Using the shadow user feature within the ALM tenant.

Questions:

  1. I have not found any documentation on configuring a scenario where a user can login to a BTP application using the login and password from a local identity provider. Is there guidance on this setup?

  2. Which of the three integrations above do you think would be the most effective for this task?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

entry.title: Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra - User not able to authenticate?
entry.content:
@MartinRaepple  @RegineSchimmer 

I would like to thank Martin Raepple | Regine Schimmerfor thier wonderful session on XP202 | Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra. 

Hi All, I was trying to replicate this issue and was able to successfully replicate to some extent.  Currently i have created a new Corporate IDP(Microsoft Entra) in the IAS system and the scenario works perfectly fine in case if the Identity Federation(User Store | User Access) is disabled. However, First name | Last name are not getting populated from the corporate IDP in the BTP subaccount user store and also user name info is populated with the subject information from the ID token. Please let me know how can we have the first name and last name populated? Ideally what info has to be populated in the User id information in BTP?

Also, ideally do we have to  enable identity federation(Only the Use Identity Authentication user store option) option or do we need to disable?

Can you please help with your comments pls!!

 

Thanks and Regards,

Maram Nagarjuna Srivatsa

entry.title: SAP Identity Management Eclipse plugin 8.8.45 not available on tools.hana.ondemand.com
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I noticed that the Eclipse plugin for SAP Identity Management, which has so far been publicly available from SAP's official Eclipse software site at https://tools.hana.ondemand.com has not been updated there for a while.

The latest Eclipse plugin version available from the public site is 8.8.41. In SAP Service Marketplace, however, the latest version is 8.8.45 already.

Is this a bug or has there been an announcement that the public download channel will be abandoned?

SAP Identity Management

entry.title: Error During User Provisioning with IPS to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned the users from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now. When I delete a user in IAS or in Azure (e.g., in the source system), the user is also deleted in the target system (Enable Now) through a provisioning job. However, when I try to provision the same user again in Enable Now, I get the following error:

user: f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef, system: Target Enable Now, time: 2024-11-21T13:09:06.302+00, error: HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-now/scim/v2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {  "status" : "400",  "scimType" : "uniqueness",  "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],   "detail" : "userUuid is or was already assigned" }, content: {  "active": true,   "emails": [{"value": "enable@mail.com"}],   "name": {"familyName": "enable", "givenName": "enable"},   "schemas": [     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"   ],  "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User": {     "userUuid": "f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef"   },  "userName": "enable@mail.com" }

When I change, for example, the email address of a user in Azure or IAS (Source System) and provision the user to Enable Now, I get the following error:

user:U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS, system=Targer Enable Now,  time=2024-11-21T1,  error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-nowp/scim/v2/Users/U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS with statusCode: 400, Response: {   "status" : "400",   "scimType" : "mutability",   "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],  

"detail" : "User auth_name is immutable" }, content={"active":true,"emails":[{"value":"enable2@mail.com"}],"id":"U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS","name":{"familyName":"enable3","givenName":"enable2"},"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"],"urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User" :{"userUuid":"3dsfg45-sdsg45-d-9ff45176-sff4ycycv"},"userName":"enable3@mail.com"}

How can I provision the deleted users again to the Target System? How can I update the changed user attributes, such as the user email, in Enable Now with a provisioning job?


Many Thanks

Best Regards










​

entry.title: IPS: Email and Telephone Not Provisioned to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Why are the email and phone number attributes not being provisioned  to Enable Now using the default transformation code?

I am provisioning users with SAP IPS  from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now.

Many Thanks

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Users and Groups in Azure and IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, we have the following scenario implemented: We have Azure as the IdP → IAS as a proxy → Cloud Apps. The groups are created in Azure, and users are assigned to those groups in Azure. These groups with their users are then provisioned to SAP IAS. From SAP IAS, the users/groups, such as SAC_Group, are provisioned to the SAC app, or the group ENABLE_Group is provisioned to Enable Now.

Since we, as the SAP team, do not have access to the Azure tenant, I am asking if there is a better approach or any best practices for managing users/groups.

Could we provision only users from Azure and create the groups directly in IAS, then manually assign users to these groups und then provision to Cloud Apps? Are there any disadvantages to this approach?

What are your experiences? How do you handle this in your environment? What are the best practices: Should users/groups be managed in Azure, or should groups be managed directly in IAS?

Which approach is better: Managing users/groups in Azure or provisioning users to IAS and managing the groups there?

Thank you so much!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards

entry.title: 'Internal error; contact system administrator' : send email to new users on Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
We import new user on CPI application with csv and then click on SEND to send the users initial onboarding email.

For past few days when we click on SEND, below error is received.

There are no changes done on SMTP settings or application.

There are no changes done to email template as well.

The 'RESET email' works and user receive email from Cloud Identity Services.

Same error is observed while setting initial password for a customer.

entry.title: SAP CDC working as OIDC RP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to set up SSO from Jainrain application to SAP CDC application, where we will be using SAP CDC as relying party (RP) and Janrain as OP. 

As per the documentation, we supplied OP with Redirect URI as ( https://socialize.eu1.gigya.com/socialize.finalizeOidcLogin?) and configured both OP and RP with details. 

Once SSO button at OP is clicked, we are triggering socialLogin call to SAP CDC. 

gigya.accounts.socialLogin ( 

provider:'oidc-myopsite', authFlow: 'redirect',    redirectURL: 'https://mywebsitelink'

);

Once we hit, accounts.sociallogin, authorize endpoint is called at OP, then it is redirected to there proxy page, which will check if session is present in OP or not, if active session is there at OP, it will redirect to, https://socialize.eu1.gigya.com/socialize.finalizeOidcLogin? along with code.

which gives, General Server Error. 

Please find below the entire call for your reference, 

https://socialize.eu1.gigya.com/socialize.finalizeOidcLogin?code=st2.s.AtLtVFxJmg.WPPZXcqHWHy03XG2-HoEZYH46NPur0VcSspI4pMXi4ppPulLGIsGnOg4XW4zLx0EnMOYHIfM7vxneZmeee5oZO1CsPkbNvQKBynLZsjo-tfwqzTKOS-1SpCrcJYByzw-.-hKQP7KZ1ck1tmMdzMcsbpmZInNPLfRbCp4qAjl8wEnz1kXRzOcfp5XU7nv7MnvTVSeucdiGqHZ657CdgGmnsw.sc3&state=tk2.oi.AtLtu3M6lQ.RXLLl3J9uWcKX3ilCougbRBq9ITlDpoccr1r-3PI4LXBjEaj1Ohh3LDQHwTWyFiJ.DuZvpX9sUObU2ROXWMdYwmZSQsL7c3iZ5lN8mRK1leGZYig1SlyOPH97bjzliOvkUXFEDd71W5SJvEaAqI7udg.sc3yc463l70n4055151h7 

Which is giving us the error,

errorMessage: 'General Server Error',
requestId: 'aac6a08e1e2747879173e47c791f91e4',

To conclude, We are trying RP initiated OIDC flow with SAP CDC working as RP, once someone clicks on SSO button at OP end, it hits accounts.socialLogin -> OP authorize end point is called -> if session is there at OP, Proxy page redirects to -> https://socialize.eu1.gigya.com/socialize.finalizeOidcLogin? along with the code, which ends in error. There is no direction given in CDC documentation to understand the flow when CDC will act as RP. 

let me know if anyone have implemented any such scenarios or use cases where you were using CDC as RP, just want to get ideas on what we are doing wrong. Thanks.

SAP Community SAP Customer Data Cloud #Authentication

entry.title: GRC PC: CAPA Plan and Remediation Plan
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can you please suggest if both CAPA Plan and Remediation Plan can be available for the same regulation. When i switch on the CAPA Plan in SPRO>..> Business Transactions.., then 'Create Remediation Plan' is not available.

Regards

Plaban
